sections.biography-source-undefined:
Li Miao was a Shu official from Qi county, Guanghan commandery. Li Miao was an older brother of Li Shao but it is unknown whether he was older or younger than another brother, Li Chao. He had a younger brother who died relatively early.

He was known for being extremely blunt in his manner, which angered many, including Liu Bei and Zhuge Liang. He once told Liu Bei, “Liu Zhang, as you were his clansman, gave you immense trust, and hence entrusted you with suppressing traitors. Your prime achievements would not have been able to be imitated, yet you first invaded and then destroyed him. I think that you seizing my province is most inappropriate.” Liu Bei said, “Knowing it to be inappropriate, why did not you not help him?” Li Miao said, “It is not because of my lack of courage, but that my strength was insufficient.” Li Miao was only able to escape execution from Liu Bei’s angry officials due to the efforts of Zhuge Liang. Soon, however, he angered Zhuge Liang by telling him that his execution of Ma Su was harmful for Shu, making historical allusions with, “Qin spared Mengming and utilized him to govern the Western Barbarians; Chu executed Ziyu and so its two generations of rulers could not compete in the world.”

When Zhuge Liang died, Li Miao sent a petition to the grieving Liu Shan which claimed Zhuge Liang had greater ambitions that threatened Liu Shan and that his death was a cause for joy for Shu since he was a warmonger. An infuriated Liu Shan promptly imprisoned and executed him. Li Miao had outlived all three of his known brothers.

full_text:
Li Miao (Hannan) 李邈 (漢南)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Li Miao (李邈), styledHannan (漢南), was born in Qi county, Guanghan commandery. Criticized Liu Bei so badly, Zhuge Liang had to save his life. Celebrated Zhuge Liang’s death. Li Miao was affiliated with Liu Zhang and the Shu Kingdom. Li Miao lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
